 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","title":"TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests (Frame Parsing, AEAD, MAC Verification)","description":"# TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove FCPS/FCPC framing, parsing, and crypto verification are correct and bounded under adversarial inputs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for wire protocol frame parsing and cryptographic verification.

## FCPS (Symbol Plane) Tests

### Frame Parsing
- [ ] Valid frame parses correctly
- [ ] Magic bytes validation ("FCPS")
- [ ] Version field bounds checking
- [ ] Flag combinations parsed correctly
- [ ] Symbol count matches actual symbols
- [ ] Total payload length matches content
- [ ] Zone ID hash is 32 bytes exactly
- [ ] Object ID is 32 bytes exactly

### Symbol Record Parsing
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) extraction
- [ ] K parameter extraction
- [ ] Data length matches symbol_size
- [ ] Auth tag is 16 bytes exactly

### Datagram Envelope
- [ ] Session ID extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] Sequence number parsing (u64 LE)
- [ ] MAC extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] MAC verification success with correct key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with wrong key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with tampered frame
- [ ] Direction byte in MAC context

### Per-Symbol AEAD
- [ ] Sender key derivation matches spec
- [ ] Nonce derivation: frame_seq || esi
- [ ] AAD binding includes all required fields
- [ ] Decrypt success with correct key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with tampered ciphertext
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong AAD

### Frame Flags
- [ ] REQUIRES_ACK flag handling
- [ ] COMPRESSED flag handling
- [ ] ENCRYPTED flag handling
- [ ] STREAMING + STREAM_END combination
- [ ] HAS_CAP_TOKEN flag handling
- [ ] ZONE_CROSSING flag handling
- [ ] CONTROL_PLANE flag handling
- [ ] Invalid flag combinations rejected

## FCPC (Control Plane) Tests

### Frame Structure
- [ ] Canonical CBOR envelope parsing
- [ ] Object type discrimination
- [ ] Length prefix validation
- [ ] Backpressure frame parsing

### Control Objects
- [ ] ControlPlaneObject type dispatch
- [ ] Session control messages
- [ ] Capability objects
- [ ] Revocation objects
- [ ] Checkpoint objects

## DoS Resistance Tests
- [ ] Oversized frame rejected
- [ ] Invalid symbol count rejected
- [ ] Frame exceeding MTU rejected
- [ ] Memory bounds on malformed input

## Golden Vectors
Each test category needs:
- Valid frame bytes (hex)
- Expected parsed structure
- Invalid frame bytes that MUST be rejected

## Fuzz Targets
- FCPS frame parsing
- FCPC frame parsing
- Symbol record parsing
- Datagram envelope parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "protocol": "fcps|fcpc",
  "operation": "parse|verify|encode",
  "frame_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error_code": "optional"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All parsing paths have test coverage
- DoS protections verified
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets find no crashes

","notes":"Tests validated: all 72 fcp-protocol tests pass. Coverage confirmed for: FCPS frame parsing/flags/symbols, FCPC framing/AEAD/replay, MAC verification, golden vectors. Fuzz targets exist for all components. Blocked waiting for 1n78.13 completion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindySnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:55:24.993533714Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added comprehensive FCPS/FCPC protocol unit tests including DoS resistance (symbol count overflow, MTU violation, memory bounds), replay protection, and reference vector generation. All 50 golden vector tests pass (28 FCPC + 22 FCPS). Implementation dependency (1n78.13) is still in progress but tests are self-contained and complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","protocol","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","title":"fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to retrieve customer context safely:
- look up users/requesters
- fetch basic profile metadata
- list recent tickets for context

This is read-heavy but touches PII.

## Scope
- Search users by email/name/external id.
- Fetch user profile (minimize returned PII).
- List tickets for a user (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.users.read`
- `zendesk.tickets.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - never log emails/phone numbers
  - return only the minimal fields required for support workflows

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.user.search`
  - `zendesk.user.get`
  - `zendesk.user.tickets.list`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - search pagination
  - redaction rules

## Acceptance criteria
- Customer context can be retrieved without leaking PII into logs.

Operation: zendesk.search_users. Covered by existing test suite.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","title":"fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library","description":"# fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library

## Overview
Unified OAuth library supporting OAuth 2.0 (authorization code, client credentials, PKCE) and OAuth 1.0a flows for connectors requiring user authorization.

## Supported Flows

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code (with PKCE)
- Authorization URL generation
- State parameter for CSRF protection
- PKCE challenge/verifier
- Token exchange
- Refresh token handling

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials
- Service-to-service authentication
- Token caching with TTL
- Automatic refresh

### OAuth 1.0a (Twitter legacy)
- Request token acquisition
- User authorization
- Access token exchange
- HMAC-SHA1 signature generation

## Token Management
- In-memory token storage (no disk!)
- Automatic refresh before expiry
- Token validation
- Secure token handling

## Provider Support
- Google (OAuth 2.0 + PKCE)
- GitHub (OAuth 2.0)
- Twitter/X (OAuth 1.0a + OAuth 2.0)
- Slack (OAuth 2.0)
- Notion (OAuth 2.0)
- Linear (OAuth 2.0)
- Custom providers via configuration

## Security
- State parameter validation
- PKCE enforcement where supported
- Secure redirect URI handling
- Token encryption at rest

## Dependencies
- reqwest, serde_json, chrono, base64
- fcp-core (for FCP integration)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:35:45.591389925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","close_reason":"Implemented fcp-oauth crate with OAuth 2.0 (authorization code + PKCE, client credentials), OAuth 1.0a, token management, and pre-configured providers. All 25 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","title":"[Registry] Implement connector registry, signing, and cross-platform binary distribution","description":"The README describes a registry architecture with remote registries, self-hosted registries, and mesh mirrors. Before execution, FCP verifies: manifest signature, binary checksum, binary signature, platform/arch match, capability subset check, and optionally transparency log + in-toto/SLSA attestations. The supply chain verification code exists in fcp-registry and fcp-evidence, but there is no running registry, no published connector binaries, and the release.yml GitHub Actions workflow has never been demonstrated. The install/update flow (fwc install, fwc update) has never been proven against real signed artifacts. This initiative creates a minimal registry and proves the distribution flow.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:49.699330Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:49.699330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","registry"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","title":"[Registry] Implement connector registry, signing, and cross-platform binary distribution","description":"The README describes a registry architecture with remote registries, self-hosted registries, and mesh mirrors. Before execution, FCP verifies: manifest signature, binary checksum, binary signature, platform/arch match, capability subset check, and optionally transparency log + in-toto/SLSA attestations. The supply chain verification code exists in fcp-registry and fcp-evidence, but there is no running registry, no published connector binaries, and the release.yml GitHub Actions workflow has never been demonstrated. The install/update flow (fwc install, fwc update) has never been proven against real signed artifacts. This initiative creates a minimal registry and proves the distribution flow.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:49.699330Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:01:42.213004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:01:42.212640Z","close_reason":"All 5 children (lopoh.1-.5) closed: signing, local registry, fwc install/update proof, release.yml Windows ARM64 matrix, supply-chain negative tests. Initiative fully reconciled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","registry"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","title":"[Registry] Sign connector binaries with Ed25519 and generate supply chain attestations","description":"Implement the connector signing workflow: 1) Build a connector in release mode, 2) Compute BLAKE3 hash of the binary, 3) Sign manifest + binary hash with publisher Ed25519 key, 4) Generate in-toto attestation linking source commit to binary hash, 5) Package as: binary + manifest.toml + signature + attestation. Verify the signing with fcp-registry's existing verification code. Output: a signed connector package suitable for registry upload. This should be scriptable as a cargo xtask or a standalone tool in crates/fcp-conformance/.","notes":"Implemented fwc supply-chain signing path with manifest signature + attestation emission and registry verification. Remote rch cargo check -p fwc passed earlier in session; targeted sign_bundle test passed remotely. Workspace cargo fmt --check is currently noisy on unrelated files outside this bead.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.276480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:27:19.462356Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:27:19.462181Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.276480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","title":"[Registry] Create minimal local registry server for connector install/update flow","description":"Build a minimal HTTP registry server that serves signed connector packages. Functionality: 1) Serve connector manifest by ID, 2) Serve connector binary by ID + platform/arch, 3) Return checksums and signatures for verification, 4) Support version listing and latest-version resolution. This does NOT need to be production-grade - it's the minimum viable registry to prove the install/update flow works. Can be implemented as a simple axum server or even a static file server with a known directory structure. Location: crates/fcp-registry/src/bin/ or a new crate.","notes":"Completed: local registry server implemented; remote rch proofs green for fcp-registry local_registry_ tests and fwc sign_bundle_emits_registry_verified_package.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.990700Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T07:34:56.237549Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T07:34:56.237380Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.990700Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:51.704147Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","title":"[Registry] Prove fwc install and fwc update against the local registry with full verification","description":"With the local registry running and signed packages available: 1) Run 'fwc install <connector>' and verify it downloads, verifies signature, checks checksum, and installs the binary, 2) Run 'fwc update <connector>' and verify it detects a newer version and upgrades, 3) Attempt install with a tampered binary - MUST fail signature verification, 4) Attempt install with wrong platform/arch - MUST fail platform check, 5) Verify supply chain attestation is checked if policy requires it. This proves the README's supply chain verification pipeline works end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.225551Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T09:19:34.695872Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T09:19:34.695697Z","close_reason":"Completed: registry install/update proof added; targeted rch check/test passed; broad rch clippy blocked by repeated worker sync timeouts to vmi1149989","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.225551Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.960837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
